description_long: This is a classic wooden barrel with a cylindrical, slightly bulging body and flat, circular ends. The exterior is constructed from vertical wooden staves, each showing a natural wood grain pattern and some weathering, giving it a rustic appearance. The barrel is reinforced by three wide, metallic hoops—two near the top and bottom edges and one at the midsection—each showing signs of oxidation and wear. The top surface is closed and flush, with no visible opening or lid, and the wood appears tightly fitted. There are no handles or spouts, and the surface is generally rough with visible seams between the staves. The overall look suggests it is designed for storage or decorative use in urban or outdoor settings.
description: A weathered wooden barrel with metal hoops, bulging sides, and a closed top, showing rustic wood grain and no handles.
description_view_0: Front view of the barrel showing the wood grain, three metal hoops, and the closed, flat top.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the curvature of the staves and the weathered texture of the wood and metal bands.
description_view_2: Another side angle, emphasizing the seamless join of the staves and the consistent placement of the metal hoops.
description_view_3: Rear view similar to the front, with visible wood grain and all three metal hoops clearly shown.
